CheckTitle: RAM user not logged on for 90 days or longer has console logon disabled
Description: **Alibaba Cloud RAM** users can log on to the console by using their username and password. If a user has not logged on for **90 days or longer**, it is recommended to disable the console access of the user. Disabling unused console access reduces the attack surface by removing unnecessary logon capabilities from potentially abandoned or dormant accounts.
Risk: Inactive accounts with console access are common targets for **account takeover**. An abandoned account or one with a **compromised password** unused for over 90 days may go unmonitored, allowing undetected unauthorized access. This risks the **confidentiality** and **integrity** of cloud resources accessible through the account.

Code.Other:
1. Log on to the **RAM Console**.
2. Choose **Identities** > **Users**.
3. In the User Logon Name/Display Name column, click the username of the target RAM user.
4. In the Console Logon Management section, click **Modify Logon Settings**.
5. In the Console Password Logon section, select **Disabled**.
6. Click **OK**.

Recommendation.Text: Disable console access for RAM users that have not logged on for 90 days or longer to reduce the attack surface.
